title: SUB POLITY 1 PREAMBLE 2 PART 1 2 OF THE CONSTITUITI

questions.question: 1. Which Article describes India as a 'Union of States'? / कौन सा अनुच्छेद भारत को 'राज्यों का संघ' बताता है?
questions.explanation: Article 1 states "India, that is Bharat, shall be a Union of States".

questions.question: 2. From which country is the concept of 'Single Citizenship' taken? / 'एकल नागरिकता' की अवधारणा किस देश से ली गई है?
questions.explanation: The concept of Single Citizenship in India is borrowed from the UK.

questions.question: 3. Which Article deals with the formation of new states or alteration of areas? / कौन सा अनुच्छेद नए राज्यों के गठन या क्षेत्रों में परिवर्तन से संबंधित है?
questions.explanation: Article 3 empowers Parliament to form new states and alter boundaries or names.

questions.question: 4. Who was the chairman of the first linguistic commission (Dhar Committee)? / प्रथम भाषाई आयोग (धर समिति) के अध्यक्ष कौन थे?
questions.explanation: The Dhar Committee (1948) was headed by S.K. Dhar.

questions.question: 5. Which was the first state created on a linguistic basis in India? / भारत में भाषाई आधार पर बनने वाला पहला राज्य कौन सा था?
questions.explanation: Andhra Pradesh became the 1st linguistic state in 1953 after Potti Sriramulu's death.

questions.question: 6. Under which operation was Hyderabad integrated into India? / किस ऑपरेशन के तहत हैदराबाद को भारत में एकीकृत किया गया था?
options: (d) Operation Blue Star / ऑपरेशन ब्लू स्टार
questions.explanation: Hyderabad was integrated through military action called Operation POLO in 1948.

questions.question: 7. Citizenship provisions are covered under which Part of the Constitution? / नागरिकता के प्रावधान संविधान के किस भाग के अंतर्गत आते हैं?
questions.explanation: Part II (Articles 5-11) of the Constitution deals with Citizenship.

questions.question: 8. Which Article empowers Parliament to regulate the right of citizenship by law? / कौन सा अनुच्छेद संसद को कानून द्वारा नागरिकता के अधिकार को विनियमित करने की शक्ति देता है?
questions.explanation: Article 11 allows Parliament to make laws regarding citizenship.

questions.question: 9. In which year was the State Reorganisation Act passed? / राज्य पुनर्गठन अधिनियम किस वर्ष पारित किया गया था?
questions.explanation: The Act was passed in 1956 based on Fazl Ali Commission recommendations.

questions.question: 10. Sikkim became a full-fledged state of India in which year? / सिक्किम किस वर्ष भारत का पूर्ण राज्य बना?
questions.explanation: Sikkim gained complete statehood in 1975 via the 36th Amendment.

questions.question: 11. What is the minimum residency period for citizenship by registration? / पंजीकरण द्वारा नागरिकता के लिए न्यूनतम निवास अवधि क्या है?
questions.explanation: Individuals must reside in India for 7 years before applying via registration.

questions.question: 12. Which Article says a person losing Indian citizenship if they voluntarily acquire foreign citizenship? / कौन सा अनुच्छेद कहता है कि स्वेच्छा से विदेशी नागरिकता लेने पर भारतीय नागरिकता समाप्त हो जाएगी?
questions.explanation: Article 9 states that voluntary acquisition of foreign citizenship ends Indian citizenship.

questions.question: 13. How many states and UTs were created by the 1956 Reorganisation Act? / 1956 के पुनर्गठन अधिनियम द्वारा कितने राज्य और केंद्र शासित प्रदेश बनाए गए थे?
questions.explanation: It created 14 States and 6 Union Territories on Nov 1, 1956.

questions.question: 14. Which Article refers to citizenship for people who migrated from Pakistan to India? / पाकिस्तान से भारत आए लोगों की नागरिकता से कौन सा अनुच्छेद संबंधित है?
questions.explanation: Article 6 provides citizenship rights to certain migrants from Pakistan.

questions.question: 15. Goa became a state of India in which year? / गोवा किस वर्ष भारत का राज्य बना?
questions.explanation: Goa became the 25th state of India in 1987.

questions.question: 16. Which committee rejected the linguistic basis for states in 1948? / 1948 में किस समिति ने राज्यों के भाषाई आधार को खारिज कर दिया था?
options: (a) Fazl Ali Commission / फज़ल अली आयोग
questions.explanation: The JVP Committee (Nehru, Patel, Sitaramayya) rejected the linguistic basis.

questions.question: 17. The 100th Constitutional Amendment (2014) is related to? / 100वां संविधान संशोधन (2014) किससे संबंधित है?
questions.explanation: It related to the exchange of enclaves between India and Bangladesh.

questions.question: 18. 'Jus Soli' refers to citizenship acquired by? / 'जूस सोली' का तात्पर्य किससे प्राप्त नागरिकता से है?
questions.explanation: 'Jus Soli' means citizenship by place of birth.

questions.question: 19. Which Article contains special provisions for the state of Nagaland? / नागालैंड राज्य के लिए विशेष प्रावधान किस अनुच्छेद में हैं?
questions.explanation: Article 371-A provides special provisions for Nagaland.

questions.question: 20. What is the total number of enclaves transferred by India to Bangladesh in the 100th CA? / 100वें संशोधन में भारत द्वारा बांग्लादेश को कितने एन्क्लेव हस्तांतरित किए गए?
questions.explanation: India transferred 111 enclaves to Bangladesh.

questions.question: 21. Which method is used to lose citizenship if it was acquired by fraud? / यदि नागरिकता धोखाधड़ी से प्राप्त की गई हो तो उसे खोने के लिए किस विधि का उपयोग किया जाता है?
questions.explanation: Deprivation occurs if citizenship was obtained by fraud or false documents.

questions.question: 22. Article 2 deals with? / अनुच्छेद 2 किससे संबंधित है?
options: (a) Formation of new states from existing ones / मौजूदा राज्यों से नए राज्यों का गठन
(b) Admission or establishment of new states / नए राज्यों का प्रवेश या स्थापना
(d) Name of the Union / संघ का नाम
questions.explanation: Article 2 talks about incorporating or establishing new foreign territories into India.

questions.question: 23. Which Princely State joined India after a referendum? / कौन सी रियासत जनमत संग्रह के बाद भारत में शामिल हुई?
questions.explanation: Junagarh became part of India after a referendum.

questions.question: 24. Who was a member of the Fazl Ali Commission? / फज़ल अली आयोग का सदस्य कौन था?
questions.explanation: Members included Justice Fazl Ali, K.M. Panikkar, and H.N. Kunzru.

questions.question: 25. Uttarakhand was created out of which state? / उत्तराखंड किस राज्य से अलग होकर बना था?
questions.explanation: Uttarakhand was created out of Uttar Pradesh in 2000.

questions.question: 26. Which Article refers to 'India, that is Bharat'? / कौन सा अनुच्छेद 'इंडिया, अर्थात भारत' को संदर्भित करता है?
questions.explanation: Article 1 defines the name and territory of the Union.

questions.question: 27. 'By Sanguine' refers to citizenship by? / 'बाय सैंग्विन' का तात्पर्य किससे नागरिकता से है?
questions.explanation: 'By Sanguine' means citizenship acquired by blood/descent.

questions.question: 28. Telengana became a state on which date? / तेलंगाना किस तारीख को राज्य बना?
questions.explanation: Telangana became the 29th state of India on June 2, 2014.

questions.question: 29. Which Amendment related to the integration of Dadra & Nagar Haveli as a UT? / दादरा और नगर हवेली को UT के रूप में एकीकृत करने से संबंधित कौन सा संशोधन था?
questions.explanation: The 10th Amendment (1961) integrated Dadra & Nagar Haveli.

questions.question: 30. Which state was created in 1966? / 1966 में किस राज्य का गठन हुआ था?
questions.explanation: Haryana was created as a separate state in 1966.

questions.question: 1. From which country is the concept of the Preamble borrowed? / प्रस्तावना की अवधारणा किस देश से ली गई है?
questions.explanation: The concept of the Preamble was borrowed from the USA.

questions.question: 2. From which country is the language of the Preamble borrowed? / प्रस्तावना की भाषा किस देश से ली गई है?
questions.explanation: While the concept is from the USA, the language is from Australia.

questions.question: 3. Who decorated and designed the Preamble? / प्रस्तावना की रचना और सजावट किसने की?
options: (a) Nand Lal Bose / नंद लाल बोस
(c) Prem Behari Narain Rayzada / प्रेम बिहारी नारायण रायज़ादा
(b) Beohar Rammanohar Sinha / व्यौहार राममनोहर सिन्हा
questions.explanation: Beohar Rammanohar Sinha decorated and designed the Preamble.

questions.question: 4. Which amendment added 'Socialist' and 'Secular' to the Preamble? / किस संशोधन ने प्रस्तावना में 'समाजवादी' और 'धर्मनिरपेक्ष' शब्द जोड़े?
questions.explanation: These words were added in 1976 during the emergency.

questions.question: 5. The Preamble is a modified version of which resolution? / प्रस्तावना किस प्रस्ताव का संशोधित संस्करण है?
options: (c) Resolution of Independence / स्वतंत्रता का प्रस्ताव
(d) Cabinet Mission Resolution / कैबिनेट मिशन प्रस्ताव
questions.explanation: It is based on the 'Objective Resolution' presented by Jawaharlal Nehru.

questions.question: 6. Who called the Preamble the 'Identity Card of the Constitution'? / प्रस्तावना को 'संविधान का पहचान पत्र' किसने कहा?
questions.explanation: N.A. Palkhivala described it as the identity card.

questions.question: 7. Who called the Preamble the 'Jewel Set' of the Constitution? / प्रस्तावना को संविधान का 'आभूषण समूह' किसने कहा?
options: (a) Pandit Thakur Das Bhargava / पंडित ठाकुर दास भार्गव
questions.explanation: Pandit Thakur Das used this term for the Preamble.

questions.question: 8. Who called the Preamble the 'Political Horoscope' of the Constitution? / प्रस्तावना को 'राजनीतिक कुंडली' किसने कहा?
questions.explanation: K.M. Munshi referred to it as the Political Horoscope.

questions.question: 9. What is the source of authority for the Indian Constitution? / भारतीय संविधान की शक्ति का स्रोत क्या है?
options: (c) We, the People of India / हम, भारत के लोग
questions.explanation: Power is derived from the people through their representatives.

questions.question: 10. When was the Indian Constitution adopted? / भारतीय संविधान कब अंगीकृत किया गया था?
questions.explanation: It was adopted and enacted on 26 Nov 1949.

questions.question: 11. What does the term 'Republic' mean? / 'गणतंत्र' शब्द का क्या अर्थ है?
options: (b) Head of state is elected / राज्य का प्रमुख निर्वाचित होता है
questions.explanation: It means the head of state is not hereditary but elected.

questions.question: 12. In which case did the SC say that the Preamble is NOT a part of the Constitution? / किस मामले में SC ने कहा कि प्रस्तावना संविधान का हिस्सा नहीं है?
options: (b) Berubari Union case / बेरुबारी संघ मामला
(a) Kesavananda Bharati case / केशवानंद भारती मामला
(c) LIC of India case / LIC ऑफ इंडिया मामला
questions.explanation: In 1960, the SC ruled it was not a part of the Constitution.

questions.question: 13. In which case did the SC rule that the Preamble IS a part of the Constitution? / किस मामले में SC ने कहा कि प्रस्तावना संविधान का हिस्सा है?
options: (a) Berubari Union case / बेरुबारी संघ मामला
(b) Kesavananda Bharati case / केशवानंद भारती मामला
(c) Minerva Mills case / मिनर्वा मिल्स मामला
(d) Shah Bano case / शाह बानो मामला
questions.explanation: The 1973 case established it as a part of the Constitution.

questions.question: 14. How many types of Justice are mentioned in the Preamble? / प्रस्तावना में कितने प्रकार के न्याय का उल्लेख है?
questions.explanation: Social, Economic, and Political justice (3 types).

questions.question: 15. The ideals of Justice are borrowed from which revolution? / न्याय के आदर्श किस क्रांति से लिए गए हैं?
questions.explanation: Social, economic, and political justice are from Russia.

questions.question: 16. How many types of Liberty are mentioned in the Preamble? / प्रस्तावना में कितने प्रकार की स्वतंत्रता का उल्लेख है?
questions.explanation: Thought, Expression, Belief, Faith, and Worship (5 types).

questions.question: 17. The ideals of Liberty, Equality, and Fraternity are from? / स्वतंत्रता, समानता और बंधुत्व के आदर्श कहाँ से हैं?
questions.explanation: These ideals were borrowed from the French Revolution.

questions.question: 18. How many types of Equality are mentioned in the Preamble? / प्रस्तावना में कितने प्रकार की समानता का उल्लेख है?
questions.explanation: Status and Opportunity (2 types).

questions.question: 19. Which word was added along with Socialist and Secular in 1976? / 1976 में समाजवादी और धर्मनिरपेक्ष के साथ कौन सा शब्द जोड़ा गया था?
questions.explanation: Integrity was the third word added by the 42nd Amendment.

questions.question: 20. What does 'Sovereign' mean? / 'संप्रभु' का क्या अर्थ है?
options: (c) Part of a colony / एक कॉलोनी का हिस्सा
(a) Under British rule / ब्रिटिश शासन के अधीन
(b) Free from external control / बाहरी नियंत्रण से मुक्त
questions.explanation: It means no external power can direct the Indian government.

questions.question: 21. What kind of economy does India have? / भारत में किस प्रकार की अर्थव्यवस्था है?
questions.explanation: India follows a mixed economic model.

questions.question: 22. Secularism in India implies: / भारत में धर्मनिरपेक्षता का तात्पर्य है:
options: (d) State supports Hindu religion / राज्य हिंदू धर्म का समर्थन करता है
(c) Only one religion is allowed / केवल एक धर्म की अनुमति है
(b) State has no official religion / राज्य का कोई आधिकारिक धर्म नहीं है
(a) State is against religion / राज्य धर्म के विरुद्ध है
questions.explanation: All religions are treated equally and fairly by the government.

questions.question: 23. Which Hindi month is mentioned in the Preamble? / प्रस्तावना में किस हिंदी महीने का उल्लेख है?
questions.explanation: Margashirsha Shukla Saptami, Samvat 2006.

questions.question: 24. How many times has the Preamble been amended? / प्रस्तावना में कितनी बार संशोधन किया गया है?
questions.explanation: It has been amended only once in 1976.

questions.question: 25. The 42nd Amendment happened during which PM's tenure? / 42वां संशोधन किस प्रधानमंत्री के कार्यकाल में हुआ था?
options: (d) Lal Bahadur Shastri / लाल बहादुर शास्त्री
questions.explanation: It occurred during the emergency under Indira Gandhi.

questions.question: 26. Is the Preamble enforceable in court? / क्या प्रस्तावना न्यायालय में प्रवर्तनीय है?
options: (d) Only in High Court / केवल उच्च न्यायालय में
(c) Only in Supreme Court / केवल उच्चतम न्यायालय में
questions.explanation: It is legally non-enforceable in a court of law.

questions.question: 27. 'Fraternity' in the Preamble ensures: / प्रस्तावना में 'बंधुत्व' सुनिश्चित करता है:
options: (a) Dignity of individual / व्यक्ति की गरिमा
questions.explanation: It ensures the dignity of individuals and the unity of the nation.

questions.question: 28. According to the LIC case (1995), Preamble is: / LIC मामले (1995) के अनुसार, प्रस्तावना है:
options: (c) Above the Constitution / संविधान के ऊपर
(b) Integral part of Constitution / संविधान का अभिन्न अंग
(a) Not a part / हिस्सा नहीं है
questions.explanation: The SC confirmed it as an integral part of the Constitution.

questions.question: 29. Equality in the Preamble refers to: / प्रस्तावना में समानता का तात्पर्य है:
options: (b) Status and Opportunity / प्रतिष्ठा और अवसर
(d) Equality of language / भाषा की समानता
(a) Equality of wealth / धन की समानता
(c) Equality of height / कद की समानता
questions.explanation: It aims to provide equal status and opportunities to all.

questions.question: 30. 'Democratic' means government of: / 'लोकतांत्रिक' का अर्थ है किसकी सरकार:
questions.explanation: Government of the people, by the people, for the people.
